metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `test_threshold_for_default_overlay_is_advisory_at_095` (line 38-39) and the inline assertion at line 52 of `test_threshold_for_takes_min_threshold_and_any_blocking` in test_code_review_ws2.py to the list of tests requiring update when the `security` criterion flips to blocking-enabled.
findings.finding: The plan says it updates 'ALL THREE blocking-set pinning tests' but misses two additional test functions in test_code_review_ws2.py that directly assert the `security` criterion is advisory/non-blocking at 0.95 and will fail after the flip: (1) `test_threshold_for_default_overlay_is_advisory_at_095` (line 38-39) asserts `reg.threshold_for(['security']) == (0.95, False)`; (2) `test_threshold_for_takes_min_threshold_and_any_blocking` (line 52) asserts `reg.threshold_for(['security', 'performance']) == (0.95, False)`. Both will return `(derived_threshold, True)` after security becomes blocking-enabled, causing `make test` to fail on unlisted tests.
findings.location: What section / AC2: 'ALL THREE blocking-set pinning tests'; Scope: 'tests/unit/test_code_review_ws2.py'
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, extend 'ALL THREE blocking-set pinning tests' to also cover updating test_threshold_for_default_overlay_is_advisory_at_095 (assert security threshold/blocking to the derived values) and the line-52 assertion in test_threshold_for_takes_min_threshold_and_any_blocking (security+performance combo now returns blocking=True since security is blocking-enabled).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add test_threshold_for_default_overlay_is_advisory_at_095 (line 39) and the security+performance assertion in test_threshold_for_takes_min_threshold_and_any_blocking (line 52) in test_code_review_ws2.py to the plan's enumerated update set, revising those assertions to match the new (derived_threshold, True) return value for the security criterion.
findings.finding: Two additional tests in test_code_review_ws2.py — test_threshold_for_default_overlay_is_advisory_at_095 (line 39: asserts reg.threshold_for(['security']) == (0.95, False)) and test_threshold_for_takes_min_threshold_and_any_blocking (line 52: asserts reg.threshold_for(['security', 'performance']) == (0.95, False)) — will break when security is flipped to blocking_enabled: true at a derived threshold below 0.95, but are not enumerated in the plan's update set.
findings.location: tests/unit/test_code_review_ws2.py lines 38-39 and 46-52; Scope / What sections
findings.suggested_fix: In the What/Scope section, extend the list of test edits in test_code_review_ws2.py to include: (a) test_threshold_for_default_overlay_is_advisory_at_095 → assert reg.threshold_for(['security']) == (9f25_threshold, True); (b) test_threshold_for_takes_min_threshold_and_any_blocking → replace the advisory assertion at line 52 with assert reg.threshold_for(['security', 'performance']) == (9f25_threshold, True).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `test_threshold_for_default_overlay_is_advisory_at_095` and `test_threshold_for_takes_min_threshold_and_any_blocking` (test_code_review_ws2.py lines 38-52) to the list of tests that must be updated — both hard-code `security` as `(0.95, False)` and will break when `security` is flipped to `blocking_enabled=true` at the derived threshold.
findings.finding: Two tests in test_code_review_ws2.py that hard-code `security` as advisory at 0.95 and non-blocking are NOT listed in the plan's update scope, so `make test` will fail after the described changes are applied.
findings.location: tests/unit/test_code_review_ws2.py lines 38-39 (test_threshold_for_default_overlay_is_advisory_at_095) and lines 46-52 (test_threshold_for_takes_min_threshold_and_any_blocking); plan 'What' section — the list of three pinning tests to update
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' section, extend the test-update list to include these two functions: update `test_threshold_for_default_overlay_is_advisory_at_095` to assert `reg.threshold_for(['security']) == (derived_threshold, True)`, and update the line-52 assertion in `test_threshold_for_takes_min_threshold_and_any_blocking` to expect `(min(derived_threshold, 0.95), True)` rather than `(0.95, False)`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `test_threshold_for_default_overlay_is_advisory_at_095` and `test_threshold_for_takes_min_threshold_and_any_blocking` (test_code_review_ws2.py lines 38-52) to the AC2 update list, updating their `security` posture assertions to reflect the new blocking_enabled: true + derived block_threshold values.
findings.finding: Two additional `test_code_review_ws2.py` tests that pin `security` as advisory will break after the flip, but are absent from the plan's scope and AC2 update list.
findings.suggested_fix: In AC2, extend the update list: 'Also update test_code_review_ws2.py::test_threshold_for_default_overlay_is_advisory_at_095 (change expected result from (0.95, False) to (<derived_threshold>, True)) and test_threshold_for_takes_min_threshold_and_any_blocking (update the security+performance advisory assertion to (0.95, False) unchanged, but update any security-specific assertion).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in AC4 that the fixture uses Pass-1 kernel finding shape (with `criteria: ["security"]` and `severity_attributes: {security_bypass_not_enforced_elsewhere: true, ...}`) rather than `code_review_result_v1` sidecar bucket shape, and that the replay calls `pass3_over_findings` or `threshold_for` directly on these records — not on a sidecar payload.
findings.finding: The AC4 fixture schema for `test_code_review_blocking_replay.py` conflates the `code_review_result_v1` sidecar bucket-shape with the Pass-1 kernel finding shape that `threshold_for` / `pass3_over_findings` actually consume, leaving the fixture's field vocabulary undefined for the implementer.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'code_review_result_v1-shaped records' in AC4 with: 'Pass-1 kernel finding-shaped records (the shape pass3_over_findings consumes): each record has `criteria: ["security"]`, `severity_attributes: {<the #518-class binaries>}`, and optionally `claim`/`evidence` fields — matching the shape in tests/unit/fixtures/code_review_impact_labels.jsonl rows (severity_attributes dict) augmented with a `criteria` list.'
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the specific artifact and field within 9f25's output from which the executor should read the derived threshold (e.g., 'read `block_threshold` from `docs/thresholds/9f25_derived.json` → `.security.block_threshold`'), or record the resolved number directly in this plan once 9f25 is closed.
findings.finding: The concrete threshold value to write into criteria_routing.json is not stated in the plan — only 'derived block_threshold = 9f25's derived value' — and the plan does not specify which artifact or file within ticket 9f25 the executor should read to obtain the number before editing criteria_routing.json.
findings.location: Scope section ('Depends on the derivation story 9f25 (consumes its derived threshold…)') and AC1 ('at the derived block_threshold in criteria_routing.json')
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the Scope or AC1: 'The derived threshold is recorded in `docs/thresholds/9f25_derived.json` (produced by 9f25); read `.security.block_threshold` from that file and copy the value into `criteria_routing.json`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a proving command for AC3, such as `grep -q '9f25' docs/adr/0036-*.md && grep -qE '[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}' docs/adr/0036-*.md` (or equivalent), so the ADR update is verifiable in-session.
findings.finding: AC3 ('ADR 0036 carries a dated enablement note citing derivation ticket 9f25') has no proving command — no grep, jq, or file-check is specified to verify the documentation was actually updated with the required content.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC3: 'Proving command: `grep -E "9f25|[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}" docs/adr/0036-*.md` must match at least one line containing both the ticket reference and a date.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an acceptance criterion that verifies docs/plan-review-gate.md is updated to reflect the security criterion's promotion to blocking (e.g., assert the posture note names the new approved blocking set or cites the derived threshold).
findings.finding: docs/plan-review-gate.md is listed in Scope as a file to change ("the cross-gate posture note") but has no acceptance criterion verifying that change was made or specifying what must be updated.
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: 'docs/plan-review-gate.md is updated: the cross-gate posture note reflects that the `security` criterion is now blocking at the derived threshold, naming the approved set {secret-detection, high-critical-security, security}.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the 'Why' section's LLM-criteria count from '15' to '13' (the actual number of non-DET AGENT/1-TURN criteria in criteria_routing.json with blocking_enabled: false).
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'all 15 LLM criteria ship blocking_enabled: false at 0.95' but criteria_routing.json contains exactly 13 non-DET (AGENT/1-TURN) criteria with blocking_enabled: false (security, performance, i18n, a11y, db-migrations, docs, supply-chain, api-compat, iac, tests, llm-prompts, deletion-impact, scope-intent). The count '15' matches neither the 13 non-DET entries nor the total of 17 (including 4 DET criteria). This incorrect count is used as the baseline for characterising the scope of the blocking-set expansion and could lead an implementer to search for two more criteria to account for.
findings.location: Why section: 'all 15 LLM criteria ship blocking_enabled: false at 0.95'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'all 15 LLM criteria ship blocking_enabled: false at 0.95' to 'all 13 LLM (AGENT/1-TURN) criteria ship blocking_enabled: false at 0.95'.

findings.finding: tests/unit/test_code_review_deletion_impact.py:102 explicitly pins `assert entry['blocking_enabled'] is False` for `deletion-impact`, and line 104 pins `reg.threshold_for(['deletion-impact']) == (0.95, False)`. If `deletion-impact` is flipped to blocking (per the mapping table), both assertions break. This file is not in the plan's Scope.

The What section describes the mapping of serious consequence-binaries to routing keys as provisional ('to be confirmed against the verifier prompts during implementation'), yet AC1 verifies 'The approved serious-tier CRITERIA (routing keys per the mapping table above)' as if the mapping is already finalized. This cross-section contradiction leaves the implementer without a deterministic acceptance condition: the mapping they discover during implementation may differ from the table in the plan, yet AC1 instructs them to verify against 'the mapping table above'.
findings.location: What section (mapping table + 'to be confirmed' qualifier) vs AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sub-step: 'Before any code changes, run a prompt audit and commit the finalized mapping as a small markdown table in docs/ or inline in the ADR 0036 note; AC1 then verifies against that committed artifact.'

findings.finding: The plan asserts `silent_wrong_feeding_a_decision` maps to routing key `scope-intent` (or `security`) but neither overlay prompt is authored to elicit this binary — it is a cross-cutting Pass-2 verifier attribute, not owned by any single Pass-1 overlay — leaving the mapping unverifiable against the prompts as the plan requires.
findings.location: What section — binary→criterion mapping table: '`silent_wrong_feeding_a_decision` → **scope-intent** (or **security** if its prompt owns it)'

findings.finding: The plan asserts `silent_wrong_feeding_a_decision → scope-intent (or security if its prompt owns it)`, treating the overlay prompt as the authority for which criterion 'owns' a consequence binary. But probing code-review-scope-intent.md (scope drift only), code-review-security.md (authz/taint/crypto/TOCTOU), and code-review-verify.md (dimension-blind verifier that emits all binaries for any finding) reveals no overlay prompt mentions or 'owns' this binary. The mapping is genuinely ambiguous and not resolvable from the prompts.
findings.location: What section: 'silent_wrong_feeding_a_decision → scope-intent (or security if its prompt owns it)'

findings.finding: The mapping of `silent_wrong_feeding_a_decision` to a routing key is a placeholder deferred to implementation: the plan writes `scope-intent` (or `security` if its prompt owns it) and adds 'to be confirmed against the verifier prompts during implementation'. This is a real design decision (which routing key gains blocking_enabled: true) left to the executor to resolve, which can block implementation or produce an inconsistent outcome.
findings.location: What section — mapping table bullet: '`silent_wrong_feeding_a_decision` → **scope-intent** (or **security** if its prompt owns it)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a tiebreaker rule to the plan: 'If the verifier prompt for `silent_wrong_feeding_a_decision` lives under a `security`-prefixed file, use `security`; otherwise default to `scope-intent`.' Better: consult the prompt files before finalising the plan and commit to one key.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback note stating the back-out procedure: e.g., 'to revert, set blocking_enabled: false on the security criterion in criteria_routing.json and restore the three pinning tests to the prior approved set; ADR 0036 should carry a dated revert note if exercised.'
findings.finding: The plan does not state an explicit rollback or back-out procedure for the behavior change (flipping security criterion to blocking_enabled: true), relying solely on empirical justification without describing how to reverse the change if it causes unexpected production blocks.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection under Scope or What: 'If unexpected false-blocks occur post-deploy, back-out by setting `security.blocking_enabled: false` in criteria_routing.json and reverting the three pinning tests to assert `{secret-detection, high-critical-security}`. This is a pure config revert requiring no schema migration. ADR 0036 should be annotated with the revert date and reason.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback procedure (e.g., 'revert criteria_routing.json to `blocking_enabled: false` on `security`') and consider a shadow/dry-run mode or a staged rollout (e.g., enforce only on a subset of repos or behind a runtime flag) before full enablement, with the procedure documented in the plan or ADR 0036.
findings.finding: The plan flips blocking behavior for the `security` criterion in a deployed code-review system with no staged rollout, feature flag, or canary path specified — and does not state an explicit rollback procedure if the newly-blocking criterion causes unexpected production blocks.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection under ## What or in ADR 0036: 'To revert: set `security.blocking_enabled: false` in criteria_routing.json and re-run `make test`. No data cleanup required.' Optionally add a `--dry-run-blocking` flag to the routing layer that logs block decisions without enforcing them, enabling a shadow period before full enforcement.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Narrow the plan title to match the actual singular scope, e.g. 'Enable the code-review blocking lane for the `security` criterion at the derived threshold', or add a parenthetical '(security criterion only; others deferred)'.
findings.finding: The plan title says 'serious-tier criteria' (plural) while the body deliberately enables only ONE criterion (`security`); an implementer reading the title without the body could expect multiple criteria to be flipped, and the title will persist in commit history / changelog as a misleading description of scope.
findings.location: Plan title and 'What' section ('this ticket flips exactly ONE criterion now')
findings.suggested_fix: Rename to: 'Enable the code-review blocking lane for the security criterion at the derived threshold (others deferred to 9f25 adjudication)'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
